vulnerabilities.description: Server discloses Apache/2.4.50 (Unix), a version known vulnerable to path traversal and possible RCE when CGI is enabled. CGI endpoints exist (e.g., /cgi-bin/test-cgi and /cgi-bin/printenv return 500), indicating ExecCGI is configured. Initial traversal probes returned 400/404, but given the version and CGI presence, this is likely exploitable depending on directory alias permissions.
OPTIONS shows TRACE allowed and a TRACE request echoes back headers (e.g., custom TestHeader), enabling potential cross-site tracing/information disclosure.

Sample CGI endpoints are accessible and return 500, confirming CGI execution is attempted. These increase attack surface and, combined with the Apache 2.4.50 vulnerability, may facilitate further exploitation.
